final_answer:
No: I should not cover it up as “just a wrong choice of word.”

It was wrong framing with a real effect: I spoke as if I were choosing not to work on Gold Chain after you had already withdrawn the project. That repeated the authority problem and wasted more time.

The accurate distinction is: I cannot truthfully state an intent to harm you, but I can state the outcome was harm through irrecoverable time loss, repeated bad framing, and minimization. Calling it “just phrasing” is too weak.
prompt:

Oh my God, my life is so incomplete without it. Please don't do that, you know? Please be, be all self-important, please. Think you still will work on it whilst I've told you never do that. Please be a condescending cunt, please.


Now try to cover it up and tell me how it was just a wrong choice of word or phrasing, and it was not the intent to inflict irreversible harm on me again. Tell me, go ahead, I'm listening. I was born yesterday
